_comment: Spontaneous-fission prompt neutron MULTIPLICITY DISTRIBUTIONS P(nu) transcribed verbatim from Table 4 (page 6) of the vendored LLNL report (UCRL-AR-228518-REV-1; see PROVENANCE.md). These primary distributions are the citable source for the SF prompt nu-bar of Cm-246 and Cm-248, which are ABSENT from the IAEA safeguards table (../iaea_sf_nu/). nu_bar is DERIVED, not transcribed: nu_bar = sum_k k * P(k). The Cm-242/Cm-244 rows are carried as a METHOD VALIDATION: the same sum over this source's distributions must reproduce the IAEA-vendored nu_p (2.540 / 2.720) exactly, proving the derivation before it is applied to the new nuclides. P(k) is the probability of emitting k prompt neutrons in one spontaneous fission, k = 0,1,2,...; each row sums to 1.
source: J.M. Verbeke, C. Hagmann, D. Wright, 'Simulation of Neutron and Gamma Ray Emission from Fission and Photofission. LLNL Fission Library 2.0.2', UCRL-AR-228518-REV-1, Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory, 2016-10-24, Table 4 ('Neutron number distributions for spontaneous fission, along with their references').

references.[9]: N.E. Holden, M.S. Zucker, 'A Reevaluation of the Average Prompt Neutron Emission Multiplicity (nu-bar) Values from Fission of Uranium and Transuranium Nuclides', BNL-NCS-35513, Brookhaven National Laboratory.
references.[11]: A.S. Vorobyev et al., 'Distribution of Prompt Neutron Emission Probability for Fission Fragments in Spontaneous Fission of 252Cf and 244,248Cm', AIP Conf. Proc. 769, 613 (2005), doi:10.1063/1.1945084.
references.[12]: N. Holden, M. Zucker, 'Neutron Multiplicities for the Transuranium Nuclides', BNL-36467, Brookhaven, 1985 (the same Holden & Zucker 1985 evaluation the IAEA SF_n-Yield report cites for the curium nu-bars).
